content:
Constraints
* Please change 10^18 to 10^15, as [[N]] never exceeds 10^15.

Cases
* Each test and its validator should cover the same scenario, but with slightly different inputs and outputs. Hence:

  • Validator 2: Please change 99 to 8 so that [[N]] is also a single-digit integer, similar to Test 2.

  • Validator 3: Please change 44444 to 700000 so that [[N]] also contains a single non-zero digit only, similar to Test 3.

  • Validator 4: Please change 900000000000000 to another [[N]] in which all digits are 9, similar to Test 4.

* Please also add a pair of new cases where [[N]] is a random number with some digits appearing more than once, e.g. 730227182.

Thanks.

Note: Several existing clashes require calculating the digital root:

https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/81598220e99b466216bf8ee272d73fc475ac
https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/6133a2ed289ce33ad363c65aa5c168ddb7a8
https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/133cc5483002893a492e9f431ab7fbc4b23

However, none of them requires determining the number of reduction steps, so this new clash can be treated as unique.
I at first thought that it was overly simple, but there is enough meat for a clash.
